data.title: Sanofi’s lunsekimig hits primary and key secondary endpoints in phase 2 Asthma and CRSwNP trials
data.description:
Sanofi’s (SNY) lunsekimig met primary and key secondary endpoints in phase 2 respiratory studies in asthma and CRSwNP, the company said on Tuesday.

Lunsekimig, a novel bispecific Nanobody VHH, is made of five linked antibody fragments designed to simultaneously block TSLP and IL-13, two separate drivers of inflammation that contribute to tissue damage in asthma and related diseases. In both studies, lunsekimig was well tolerated, with an acceptable safety profile, the statement said.

“These data are promising and support our belief that the dual-targeting mechanism of lunsekimig may offer a novel treatment option for patients living with respiratory diseases, including asthma,” Houman Ashrafian, executive vice president, head of research & development at Sanofi (SNY) said.

“Importantly, these findings underscore lunsekimig’s potential to address multiple critical aspects of respiratory disease management through its unique mechanism.”

data.title: Spain's service sector growth sustained in March
data.description:
• The headline S&P Global Spain Services PMI improved to 53.3 in March. That was up from51.9 in February to signal a stronger rate of activity growth. 
  • The S&P Global Spain Composite PMI registered 52.4 in March, up from 51.5 in the previous month. 
  • "Spain’s service sector expanded at a solid rate in March, with growth picking up on February’s low. However, despite this improvement, when combined with a downturn in manufacturing output in March, Spain’s economy has experienced a weaker growth profile overall in the first quarter of 2026. Expect, therefore, official data on GDP for early 2026 to show a slower rate of expansion than the 0.8% quarterly gain reported for the fourth quarter of 2025." said Paul Smith, Economics Associate Director at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

data.title: Horizon Petroleum upsizes convertible debenture private placement to $4M
ING calls off sale of Russian bank, plans to exit Russia
data.description:
ING (ING) terminated the agreement to sell ING Bank (Eurasia) JSC to Global Development JSC after assessing there was no realistic expectation the buyer would obtain necessary approvals, the company said on Tuesday, adding that it sees no future in Russia and remains focused on ending activities there.

The company expects a financial impact similar to the earlier proposed sale, with ~7 basis points negative CET1 ratio impact.

Since February 2022, ING has taken on no new business with Russian clients and has scaled down operations and has taken actions to separate its business in Russia from ING’s networks and systems.

The company said it will also continue to further reduce offshore exposure to Russian clients. This exposure, which is booked by ING entities outside of Russia, has decreased by almost 90% to €0.6 billion as of year-end 2025, of which €0.3 billion is under ECA or CPRI cover.

data.title: Four Corners Property Trust secures $200M seven-year term loan facility; draws $50M for pipeline
Quant check on insurer stocks as Medicare Advantage rate boost drives gains
data.description:
As the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services announced that the average Medicare Advantage payments will be boosted by 2.48% in 2027, major healthcare insurers sharply moved higher in after-hours trading on Monday.

The CMS payment rate is closely watched by the market and dictates how much insurers can charge for monthly premiums and benefits, directly influencing their profitability. Previously in January, the administration had proposed a much smaller 0.09% hike, which had pressured insurers’ stocks.

With a shift in sentiment translating to stock levels, Quant ratings provide a snapshot of which insurance provider names are screening strongest.

Here is the list:

  1. HealthEquity (HQY), Quant Rating: 3.57 

  2. Centene Corporation (CNC), Quant Rating: 3.38 (up 4.3% after-hours on Monday)

  3. Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Quant Rating: 3.30 (up 12.5% after-hours on Monday)

  4. UnitedHealth Group (UNH) , Quant Rating: 3.32 (up 7.8% after-hours on Monday)

  5. Progyny (PGNY), Quant Rating: 3.08 (up 1.1% after-hours on Monday) 

  6. Elevance Health (ELV), Quant Rating: 2.97 (up 6.7% after-hours on Monday)

  7. Humana (HUM), Quant Rating: 2.78 (up 11% after-hours on Monday)

  8. Molina Healthcare (MOH), Quant Rating: 2.61 (up 3.8% after-hours on Monday)

ETFs to track: (IXJ), (VHT), (XBI), (XLV), (RSPH) (KIE), (IAK).

data.title: Asia markets muted amid uncertainty as Trump’s Iran infrastructure deadline looms
data.description:
Asian equity markets traded in narrow ranges on Tuesday as investors prepared for a key deadline in the Middle East conflict. President Donald Trump set a final Tuesday ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on civilian infrastructure. Iran rejected the latest 45-day ceasefire proposal, insisting on a permanent end to hostilities and lifting sanctions as non-negotiable conditions.

Gold prices traded around $4,650 per ounce on Tuesday after falling for two straight sessions.

WTI crude futures climbed above $115 per barrel on Tuesday, trading near their highest close since June 2022.

The benchmark KOSPI rose more than 1% to around 5,460 on Monday. The South Korean  

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.18% to above 53,700, while the broader Topix gained 1% to 3,681 on Tuesday. The Japanese yen weakened toward 160 per dollar on Tuesday, reaching levels last seen in July 2024.

In Japan, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said she plans talks with Iran’s leader and a separate call with Trump amid efforts to find a path toward peace.

China's (SHCOMP) rose 0.21% to 3,899 on Tuesday, while the Shenzhen Component Index gained 0.4% to 13,409, with both benchmark indices rebounding from losses in the previous week, and the offshore yuan weakened to around 6.88 per dollar on Tuesday, snapping a two-session gain.

Hong Kong (HSI) stock markets closed.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.35% to 73,720 on Tuesday, retreating from gains in the previous session.

Adding to market volatility, the Reserve Bank of India’s rate decision on Wednesday is in focus, with interest rates expected to remain steady at 5.25%, but guidance remains key. 

Australia (AS51) rose 1.59% to a four-week top of 8,753 in early deals Tuesday. The Australian dollar remained weak around $0.690, staying near two-month lows.

The S&P Global Australia Services PMI Business Activity Index fell to 46.3 in March 2026, larger than initial estimates of a 46.6 contraction, down from 52.8 in February, marking the first contraction in 26 months and the sharpest decline since November 2023.

Australia’s Monthly Inflation Gauge, compiled by the Melbourne Institute, climbed 1.3% month-on-month in March 2026, reversing a 0.2% drop in the previous month.

ANZ-Indeed Australian Job Ads slipped 3.1% month-on-month in March 2026, reversing February’s 16-month high of 3.2% and signaling the first decline in three months.

Household spending in Australia rose 0.3% month-on-month in February 2026, matching January’s pace and marking a second straight month of growth. 

In the U.S. on Monday, all three major indexes ended higher amid hopes for a Middle Eastern ceasefire. 

Investors are now looking to the latest Federal Open Market Committee minutes for further guidance on the central bank’s policy outlook.

U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday as investors braced for President Donald Trump’s looming deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz: Dow -0.23%; S&P 500 -0.42%; Nasdaq -0.54%.

In the U.S., attention will turn to the FOMC minutes, CPI data, the ISM Services PMI, the preliminary Michigan Consumer Sentiment reading, and the PCE report. 

Investors will also look to fresh corporate earnings, including results from Delta Air Lines, as the earnings season gathers momentum mid-month. 

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: Pershing Square offers €9.4B to acquire all shares of Universal Music Group
data.description:
Pershing Square Capital Management submitted a non-binding proposal to buy all outstanding shares of Universal Music Group N.V., offering €9.4B in cash, or €5.05 per share, and 0.77 shares of the new UMG stock per existing share.

All transaction equity financing will be backstopped by Pershing Square and affiliates, and all debt financing will be committed at signing, the company said in a filing.

Total consideration package of cash and stock estimated to be worth €30.40 per share, a 78% premium to UMG’s stock price. The transaction will facilitate the cancellation of 17% of UMG shares outstanding while preserving the company’s investment-grade balance sheet.

Pershing Square said UMG’s stock has lagged due to uncertainty over Bolloré’s stake, delayed U.S. listing, and unused balance sheet potential. "The absence of a publicly disclosed capital allocation plan and earnings algorithm" undervalued Spotify (SPOT) shares, and weak investor relations also weighed on performance, it added.

In the transaction, UMG will merge with Pershing Square SPARC Holdings, and the newly merged company will become a Nevada corporation (“New UMG”), listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: Starbucks adds energy-boosted refreshers to menu
data.description:
• Starbucks (SBUX) launched new energy refreshers in U.S. stores from April 7, boosting classic flavors with natural caffeine and B vitamins, the company said on Monday.
  • All refreshers can now also be made caffeine-free.

data.title: Toyota overtakes BYD in Japan EV sales with $6,000 subsidy advantage
data.description:
• Toyota (TM) has gained a strong edge over BYD (BYDDF) (BYDDY) in Japan's EV market, driven by favorable government subsidies and robust sales of its updated bZ4X SUV, according to a Nikkei report.
  • The government subsidies for its models increased by roughly $6,000 compared with the Chinese automaker, giving Toyota (TM) a significant pricing advantage for consumers.
  • Under Japan’s updated EV incentive program, Toyota (TM) models qualify for up to ¥1.3 million ($8,188), while BYD’s (BYDDF) vehicles remain eligible for only ¥350,000–¥450,000 ($2,204–$2,834). This gap of nearly $6,000 per vehicle significantly alters the cost equation for buyers.
  • BYD (BYDDF) had previously led Japan’s EV market in 2024, selling approximately 2,223 vehicles compared with Toyota’s (TM) 2,038.
  • Furthermore, Toyota (TM) sold over 2,000 bZ4X units in Japan in February 2026, its fourth straight month as the top domestic EV.
